title: Secrets belong in env vars, never in checked-in config
description: An API key landed in a JSON config file that got committed and pushed. Had to rotate the key and rewrite history.
context: Config file supported both env-var substitution and literal values; the literal path was easier so it won.
resolution: Rotated the key, force-pushed a cleaned history, added a pre-commit hook scanning for high-entropy strings.
prevention: Any field whose name matches `*_KEY`, `*_TOKEN`, `*_SECRET`, `*_PASSWORD`, or `*_DSN` must reference an env var (`${VAR_NAME}`) — never a literal value. Verify before committing any config file.
trigger: About to edit or create a config file that contains an authentication-related field
